Thank you for your understanding and cooperation! 🙏

CBOR `UByteArraySerializer` encodes as signed bytes

**Describe the bug**
The CBOR `UByteArraySerializer` encodes as signed bytes rather than unsigned. Only tested with `UByteArraySerializer` but may affect other unsigned encoding.

**To Reproduce**
```kotlin
Cbor.encodeToHexString(UByteArraySerializer(), ubyteArrayOf(208u))
    .also(::println)
```

**Expected behavior**
```
9f18d0ff
```

_Which [cbor.me] shows as:_

```
9F       # array(*)
   18 D0 # unsigned(208)
   FF    # primitive(*)
```

**Actual behavior**
```
9f382fff
```

_Which [cbor.me] shows as:_

```
9F       # array(*)
   38 2F # negative(47)
   FF    # primitive(*)
```


**Environment**
 - Kotlin version: 2.3.20
 - Library version: 1.11.0
 - Kotlin platforms tested on: JVM, JS, MacOS ARM64, WASM
 - Gradle version: 9.4.0

[cbor.me]: https://cbor.me/

Library jar is not included to war

Hi, 
After upgrade to kotlin 2.2.21 from 2.0.20, ktor 3.3.3 after the WAR build coroutines-core is not added. Due to that I can not start application on Open shift. 

2026-02-25 10:23:19.643 SEVERE [http-nio-8080-exec-1] org.apache.catalina.core.StandardWrapperValve.invoke Allocate exception for servlet [KtorServlet]
java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope


I use dependencies in build: 
implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-core:$kotlinxCoroutinesVersion")

KotlinxCoroutinesVersion=1.10.2

I can see that dependency in RuntimeClasspath.

Allow JsonClassDiscriminator to reuse existing field

Hello.

I was checking the docs of the library and, in fhe part regarding the polymorphism, I realized that `JsonClassDiscriminator` doesn't allow to use an existing field to discriminate between the different subtypes like, for example, Jackson allows with https://fasterxml.github.io/jackson-annotations/javadoc/2.4/com/fasterxml/jackson/annotation/JsonTypeInfo.As.html#EXISTING_PROPERTY.

I've a Kotlin library modeling an our internal binary protocol where, every block, is represented with its own class with an `id` not mutable property and even polymorphic types have to have such a property set which is, of course, different for each and every block/class.

If the same feature like in Jackson would be supported, I could potentially avoid to add the class names when serializing the polymorphic types which is, in my opinion, even safer since a class name may change.

Add @JvmStatic to generated serializer() in Companion

**What is your use-case and why do you need this feature?**

The `serializer()` method that gets added/generated in a `companion` object of a class is not marked with `@JvmStatic`.
While it works with no issues in Kotlin, in Java its UX is very unpleasant since we've to do `MyType.Companion.serializer()` all the time. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Mark the generated method with `@JvmStatic` so that `MyType.serializer()` is enough also when the library is used in a Java project.
